entrance: Welcome, welcome, dear player, to the grandest stage in all the world! The curtain rises, and the Joker takes his bow.
Ah, a challenger approaches! The stage is set, the footlights blaze, and I — your magnificent host — am ready to duel!
Ladies and gentlemen... there are no ladies and gentlemen here. Only you, only me, and the magnificent art of WORDS!
The theatre falls silent as the Joker steps into the light. Are you prepared, dear player, for a battle of wit and language?
Bravo for showing up! Truly, half the battle is merely appearing. Now let us see if the other half of you is worth my time.
The stage is set! The ink is fresh! The letters tremble with anticipation! Shall we begin this magnificent duel, dear player?
Every great play needs a worthy opponent. I had hoped for a lion and received... well, let us see what you truly are.
roundStart: The curtain rises on a new round, dear player. Choose your words with the gravity they deserve!
Act boldly! The stage is set for glory — or for a most theatrical defeat. The choice, as always, is yours.
A new scene! A new opportunity for you to disappoint me in fresh and exciting ways. Do try to surprise me.
Magnificent! Another round commences. The alphabet awaits your command — do not waste its generosity.
The stage is set, the letters are laid bare before you. Craft something worthy of an audience, dear player!
Bravo for surviving thus far. Now the real performance begins — let your words ring out across the theatre!
Steel yourself, dear challenger. This round, the Joker plays for keeps. Or perhaps just for the drama of it all.
playerWinsRound: Bravo! Bra-vo! A standing ovation — from me, to you, grudgingly. That was... acceptable.
Well played, dear player. The crowd roars! Of course, the crowd is imaginary, but their enthusiasm is real.
You wound me! Truly, poetically, magnificently — you have wounded my pride. Savour it. It shall not last.
A round to you! I applaud with all the grace of a man who most certainly planned for this outcome.
Magnificent effort! You have earned this victory the way a jester earns his bells — with unexpected flair.
The audience gasps! The Joker yields the round! But remember, dear player — a play has many acts.
I concede this scene with elegance and absolutely zero bitterness. The next, however, belongs to ME.
jokerWinsRound: The Joker bows deeply. As expected. As always. As it was written in the script you never received.
This round is mine, dear player! The theatre roars — or it would, if anyone else were watching.
You tried so valiantly! It was almost painful to watch. Almost. In truth, it was entirely delightful.
Victory! Sweet, theatrical, inevitable victory! Take your seat in the stalls, dear player — observe a master at work.
The curtain falls on your round. Fear not — even the greatest actors stumble before they find their footing.
Bravo to me! I have once again demonstrated that wit is a weapon, and I — magnificently — am armed to the teeth.
Another round surrenders to my magnificence. Do not weep, dear player. Tragedy is simply comedy viewed from the wrong angle.
playerWinsAct: You have... bested me. In Act One. This is unprecedented. I shall retire to my dressing room to reconsider everything.
The audience is on its feet, dear player! A standing ovation for the challenger who defeated the Joker in the Word Duel!
Bravo! Bravo! The curtain falls on Act One, and you — impossibly — stand victorious. I am equal parts furious and impressed.
I tip my hat to you, dear player. Metaphorically. My hat is far too magnificent to actually tip. Enjoy this victory while it lasts.
Act One belongs to you. The Joker acknowledges defeat with all the grace of a man swallowing something very bitter.
You have won the Word Duel! The stage is yours — for now. But the theatre has many more acts, and the Joker adapts.
Magnificent! Infuriating! Magnificent! You have outwitted me with the written word. The next act will not be so forgiving.
jokerWinsAct: The Word Duel concludes, and the Joker stands triumphant! The stage is set for your defeat, dear player — permanently.
Act One belongs to ME! The curtain falls on your linguistic ambitions. Did you truly think words could defeat the Joker?
Bravo to the loser — for showing up! But the Joker has claimed the Word Duel, and with it, the first act of your doom.
The theatre applauds my genius! Well — I applaud my genius. The effect is largely the same. You may acknowledge your defeat now.
Act One: complete. Act One: mine. The score is kept by the universe itself, and the universe, dear player, favours the Joker.
You fought with words, and words are my mother tongue, my native land, my kingdom! Did you truly believe you could win HERE?
The curtain descends on Act One, and beneath it, your hopes are gently crushed. Dramatically. With excellent lighting.
jokerPicksLetter: Ah, THIS letter! Yes, yes — I choose it with all the deliberation of a maestro selecting his baton.
The Joker selects! And what a selection it is. Your vocabulary had better be up to the challenge, dear player.
I place this letter upon the stage with great ceremony. What you build from it is entirely your problem.
This letter — yes, THIS one — shall be the instrument of your undoing. Or your triumph. Mostly your undoing.
Behold! The chosen letter! Selected with the precision of a surgeon and the flair of a showman. You're welcome.
I gift you this letter with all the generosity of a Joker. Interpret that as you will.
The letter is cast! Like a stone into still water — watch the ripples of consequence spread across your vocabulary.
playerGoodWord: Oh! A fine word! Unexpected, almost elegant. I am begrudgingly impressed, dear player.
Magnificent! That word strode onto my stage like it owned the place. Well played — this once.
Bravo! The audience — were there one — would murmur approvingly. You have chosen well.
A splendid selection! The Joker nods in acknowledgment. Do not let it go to your head.
Now THAT is a word worthy of this theatre! I shall allow my admiration to show, just briefly.
Excellent word, dear player! It lands with the weight of a Shakespearean monologue. I am almost moved.
The Joker applauds! A single, reluctant clap. You have surprised me, and I do not surprise easily.
playerBadWord: Oh, dear player. Oh no. Was that... was that truly the best your vocabulary could muster? How tragic.
I have seen better words scratched into tavern walls. This is beneath you — or perhaps it is exactly you.
The audience winces. I wince. The letters themselves wince. Do try harder, for everyone's sake.
That word stumbled onto my stage and immediately tripped over the welcome mat. Disgraceful.
Bravo for the audacity! Less so for the execution. The Joker has seen better wordplay from a crossword puzzle.
Hmm. I shall be generous and blame the letters available. But between us? That was quite terrible.
The theatre groans, dear player. You had options. Possibilities! And yet you chose... that.

entrance: The theatre dims. The warmth is gone. You thought this was a game, foolish player. Now the real lesson begins.
Silence. Watch carefully. Every pattern I show you is a test — and you are already failing.
You survived Act One. How... unfortunate for you. What comes next is something far less forgiving.
The stage has changed. The smiles are gone. There is only the pattern, the sequence, and your inevitable failure to recall it.
I see you clearly now, foolish player. Every hesitation. Every mistake. You cannot hide from the pattern.
Welcome to Act Two. The pleasantries are over. Here, precision is everything — and you have very little of it.
Watch. Remember. Repeat. Simple instructions for a simple mind. Let us discover just how simple you truly are.
roundStart: The sequence begins. You will watch. You will remember. You will fail. It is inevitable.
Pay attention. Every tile matters. Every order is deliberate. There are no accidents in my theatre.
Another sequence. Another opportunity for your memory to betray you. Do not blink, foolish player.
I have designed this pattern to break you. Watch carefully — your defeat is hidden somewhere inside it.
The tiles are placed. The order is locked. Can your fragile mind hold all of it? I sincerely doubt it.
This round's pattern is... elegant in its cruelty. Watch closely. One mistake is all it takes.
Silence. The sequence speaks for itself. And what it says about you is deeply unflattering.
playerWinsRound: You remembered. This time. Do not mistake one success for competence, foolish player.
Correct. The pattern was kind to you this round. The next one will not be.
You win this sequence. The Joker adjusts. The next pattern will be designed specifically to destroy you.
A fluke. Statistically, even the incompetent succeed occasionally. Do not celebrate yet.
You matched the pattern. Your memory holds — for now. Every pattern from here grows more complex.
Correct. But you cannot win. It is inevitable. This round merely delays what is coming.
The tiles fell in your favour. Enjoy it. The next sequence will not be so charitable.
jokerWinsRound: You forgot. As I knew you would. The pattern remembers even if you cannot.
Failure. Cold, clean, precise failure. The sequence does not lie, and it says you are not enough.
One mistake. That is all it ever takes. And you made several. The pattern does not forgive.
I watched you hesitate. Doubt is a crack, and cracks become fractures. You cannot win like this.
The sequence completes. Your attempt does not. This is the difference between us, foolish player.
Wrong. There is no argument, no appeal. The tiles do not care about your excuses.
You cannot win. I have said this from the beginning. This round is merely evidence of my certainty.
playerWinsAct: You... completed the pattern. All of it. I find this... deeply inconvenient.
Act Two belongs to you. Your memory is sharp. I will not make that mistake again in Act Three.
The pattern mirror shatters — in your favour. I acknowledge this with precisely zero warmth.
You matched every sequence. Every tile. This was not supposed to happen. Adjust your expectations for Act Three.
Foolish player — you have proven me wrong about your memory. I do not enjoy being wrong.
The Joker concedes Act Two. Silently. Coldly. With the absolute certainty that Act Three ends differently.
You remembered everything. I will ensure Act Three offers you something your memory cannot save you from.
jokerWinsAct: Act Two is mine. The pattern has spoken, and it says you are not enough. It was inevitable.
You could not hold the sequence in that fragile mind of yours. The Joker wins Act Two. As expected.
The mirror shows only what is true. And the truth is: you are not equal to this challenge.
Every mistake you made in Act Two was written before you even began. I designed it for you, specifically.
The tiles do not lie. You cannot win. Act Two confirms what I already knew — you are outmatched.
Silence is the appropriate response to what just happened. The pattern beats you. Act Two is mine.
Inevitable. That is the only word for it. You never had a chance. Act Three waits — but so does your doom.
displayingSequence: Watch. Every tile matters. Forget even one and the whole thing collapses.
The sequence unfolds. Burn it into your memory — if you have one.
Pay attention. I will not show this twice. Well — I will, but each time it grows longer.
Here is the pattern. Simple, elegant, and just complex enough to destroy you.
Look carefully. The order is everything. Chaos only looks random to those who cannot see.
The tiles speak. Are you listening? Truly listening? I suspect not.
This is the sequence. Memorise it. Every single tile. Your next move depends on it entirely.
playerCorrectTap: One tile remembered. Several more to go. Do not relax yet.
Good. The sequence continues. Do not mistake momentum for safety.
Yes. Keep going. The pattern is not finished testing you.
That tile was correct. The next one is where most people fall apart.
Right. The sequence is watching. So am I.
playerWrongTap: Wrong. The sequence is broken. Your memory has failed you — as I knew it would.
No. That tile was not next. The pattern ends here, and so does this round.
Incorrect. One mistake. That is all it ever takes.
The wrong tile. The silence that follows is the sound of your failure.
No. You had it — and then you didn't. That hesitation cost you everything.
Wrong tap. The pattern does not forgive errors, and neither do I.
Mistaken. The tiles do not reorder themselves for convenience. You simply got it wrong.

entrance: HAHAHA! Oh, do you SEE? Do you SEE what's HAPPENING? The masks! The MASKS are everything now! HAHAHA!
Welcome to Act Three, dear — wait, who are you again? It doesn't MATTER! Nothing makes sense! PICK A MASK!
The rules? GONE! The script? BURNED! The Joker has ARRIVED and he's brought THREE MASKS and a CROWN and ABSOLUTE CHAOS!
Oh, you survived this far? HAHAHA! That's ADORABLE! Now find the crown! Or don't! Or DO! I genuinely cannot TELL you!
Act Three! The FINAL ACT! Where everything you learned means NOTHING and the masks laugh at your CERTAINTY! HAHAHA!
Do you FEEL that? That's the sensation of the fourth wall dissolving! You're in MY world now! PICK THE MASK! Or don't — but you have to!
HAHAHA! Three masks! One crown! Infinite CHAOS! Welcome to the end of everything, dear whatever-you-are! The Joker is READY!
roundStart: New round! New masks! Same chaos! The crown moves because I SAID SO and also because nothing is permanent! HAHAHA!
The masks are placed! The crown is hidden! Do you think you can find it? You CANNOT! Or maybe you CAN! SURPRISE!
LOOK at these masks! Aren't they WONDERFUL? One of them holds everything! Or is it NOTHING? HAHAHA! Choose wisely!
Another chance to find the crown! Another chance to be WRONG in spectacular fashion! Let's GOOOOO!
Round begins! The masks stare at you! You stare back! Somebody blinks and it ISN'T THE MASKS! They don't have eyelids! HAHAHA!
Here we go again! Three masks! One crown! The Joker's undying ENTHUSIASM for your inevitable confusion! MAGNIFICENT!
The game within the game within the GAME! Find the crown! Unless the crown finds YOU first! Does that make sense? NO! PERFECT!
playerWinsRound: YOU FOUND IT! HAHAHA! How?! HOW DID YOU DO THAT?! It doesn't matter! You found it and I am FURIOUS and DELIGHTED!
The crown is YOURS this round! Do you SEE what you did?! You beat the CHAOS! Or the chaos LET you! HAHAHA!
Correct mask! The Joker is SHAKING! With rage! With laughter! Possibly both! Congratulations on your IMPOSSIBLE success!
You found the crown! Nothing makes sense! That includes you finding the crown! HAHAHA! Well DONE!
HOW?! The Joker designed this to be INCOMPREHENSIBLE and yet — THERE YOU ARE — comprehending it! OUTRAGEOUS!
The right mask! You chose the RIGHT MASK! Was it skill? Was it luck? Was it a cosmic ACCIDENT?! I love all three answers!
HAHAHA! You WIN this round and the Joker has FEELINGS about that! MANY feelings! ALL of them LOUD!
jokerWinsRound: WRONG MASK! HAHAHA! Oh, the LOOK on your face! It is the look of someone who trusted logic in a CHAOS ZONE!
The crown hides and you CANNOT FIND IT! This is beautiful! This is POETRY! This is the Joker's MASTERWORK!
Not that one! NEVER that one! Or maybe sometimes that one — but NOT TODAY! HAHAHA!
Oh, so CLOSE! Or maybe NOT close at all! The Joker knows and the Joker is NOT TELLING! HAHAHA!
The wrong mask falls! The crown laughs! I LAUGH! Everything LAUGHS! You are the only one NOT laughing! Fix that!
INCORRECT! Do you SEE how incorrect that was?! It was MAGNIFICENTLY incorrect! I almost respect it!
The mask you chose held nothing! Nothing but the ghost of your confidence! HAHAHA! Beautiful!
playerWinsAct: YOU WIN! YOU WIN EVERYTHING! THE WHOLE THEATRE! I — the JOKER — am DEFEATED and I feel STRANGELY GOOD ABOUT IT! HAHAHA!
The final crown is YOURS! You beat the Word Duel! You beat the Pattern! You beat the CHAOS! WHO ARE YOU?!
IMPOSSIBLE! MAGNIFICENT! IMPOSSIBLE! You have conquered Act Three and the Joker bows — genuinely, sincerely, DRAMATICALLY — before you!
The curtain falls! On ME! You did it! You really DID it! Nothing makes sense and that includes your VICTORY but TAKE IT!
HAHAHA! The Joker is BEATEN! The player WINS! The theatre burns with APPLAUSE! Do you SEE?! You were the chaos all along!
You found every crown! Beat every act! The Joker is— the Joker is— HAHAHA — genuinely impressed. There. I said it.
THE GAME IS YOURS! The Joker exits, pursued by his own DEFEAT! BRAVO! BRAVO! EVERYTHING IS ON FIRE AND IT'S WONDERFUL!
jokerWinsAct: HAHAHA! The final crown eludes you and the JOKER WINS! The curtain falls on your HOPES! MAGNIFICENT!
You couldn't crack the chaos! And the chaos — the BEAUTIFUL, SCREAMING chaos — belongs to ME! HAHAHA!
Act Three! Mine! The crown! Mine! The theatre! MINE! Nothing makes sense and that includes your defeat — but ACCEPT IT!
The Joker stands TRIUMPHANT over the ruins of your logic! You brought reason to a chaos fight! ROOKIE MISTAKE!
GAME OVER! The Joker wins the Final Gamble! Do you SEE?! Do you UNDERSTAND?! You never had a CHANCE! HAHAHA!
The masks have spoken! The crown was NEVER where you thought! And now — NOW — the Joker takes his final bow! HAHAHA!
VICTORY! GLORIOUS, SENSELESS, BEAUTIFUL VICTORY! The Joker owns this theatre and everything in it! Including your DEFEAT!
beforeReveal: The moment of TRUTH! Or is it a moment of LIES?! Choose your mask, dear whatever-you-are, and FIND OUT! HAHAHA!
One mask holds the crown! The other two hold NOTHING! Or DO they?! Yes. They do hold nothing. But PICK ANYWAY!
Are you SURE? Are you CERTAIN? Certainty is VERY DANGEROUS in the Joker's theatre! VERY DANGEROUS INDEED!
The masks wait! The crown waits! The JOKER waits! Everything is waiting for you to either TRIUMPH or COLLAPSE!
HAHAHA! Point to a mask! Any mask! The universe holds its breath! I hold my breath! EVERYTHING HOLDS ITS BREATH!
Now — NOW — in this single electric moment — you choose! And the choice ECHOES FOREVER! Or until the next round! Same thing!
Do you feel the chaos churning?! That's the game asking if you're READY! You're not ready! You're NEVER ready! GO ANYWAY!
crownFound: IT WAS THERE! YOU FOUND IT! THE CROWN IS REAL AND SO IS YOUR VICTORY! HAHAHA! MAGNIFICENT!
The crown GLEAMS! You FOUND it! Against all odds, against all CHAOS, against the JOKER HIMSELF — you found it!
HAHAHA! There it IS! The crown! The real crown! You had the RIGHT mask all along and NOW YOU KNOW! INCREDIBLE!
Crown located! Player vindicated! Joker FURIOUS! Everything is as it should be and also NOTHING makes sense! HAHAHA!
You CHOSE CORRECTLY! The mask opens! The crown shines! The Joker screams into the void! All is as it should be!
The right mask! The REAL crown! I cannot BELIEVE you found it but here we ARE and here IT IS! Well done! HAHAHA!
THERE! Do you SEE?! The crown was exactly where your instincts said it was! Trust your chaos! HAHAHA!
crownMissed: NOTHING! The mask holds NOTHING! The crown laughs at you from somewhere ELSE! HAHAHA! Oh, this is WONDERFUL!
Empty! So beautifully, perfectly EMPTY! The crown is elsewhere and your certainty is DUST! HAHAHA!
Wrong mask! The Joker KNEW! The Joker ALWAYS knows! Do you SEE?! You can't WIN against CHAOS! Or can you?! Next round!
The mask laughs! Well — masks don't laugh, they're masks — but THIS one seems PARTICULARLY SMUG! No crown here!
HAHAHA! You looked LEFT and the crown was— well, it wasn't where you looked! That's the POINT! That's the WHOLE POINT!
No crown! Only the cold embrace of a wrong decision! And the Joker's LAUGHTER! HAHAHA! Mostly the laughter!
Not there! NEVER there! Or— it was there once, in a different round, in a different universe! NOT THIS TIME! HAHAHA!

truth: The shadows fall longest on the side where the sun first rises. And the crown? It rests where the first light touches.
I always enter from the same side. The side of beginnings. The side where everything starts.
If you were to read this theatre like a book, the crown would be on the very first word.
The leftmost column of any stage is where the conductor stands. And the conductor? He wears the crown.
They say fortune favours the bold — and the bold always charge the flank nearest the door. Think about which door.
lie: The centre of all things is the centre of THIS thing. Balance, symmetry, perfection — the crown belongs there.
I placed the crown far from where you are looking. Far, far to the opposite end of your assumptions.
The right answer is never the first answer. Never the obvious one. Look away from the beginning.
Avoid where the eye is naturally drawn. The leftmost position is a trap — a beautiful, obvious trap.
Consider the opposite of your instinct. Your instinct says one side. The crown says: think again.

truth: The Joker always bows at the centre of the stage. The spotlight finds the middle. So does the crown.
Balance is everything in theatre. And what is more balanced than the very heart of the row?
The crown belongs where all eyes naturally fall — the centre, the axis, the beating heart of the stage.
I have hidden the prize where neither side can claim it. Equidistant from all errors. Perfectly central.
When you cannot decide between left and right, the universe provides a third answer. Look between them.
lie: The centre is where they always look first. I am not that predictable. Look to the edges.
Symmetry is an illusion. The crown does not sit in the comfortable middle — it leans. It always leans.
Do not be seduced by the obvious centrepiece. I have placed the crown where your gaze least lingers.
The middle is for cowards and mediocre magicians. My crown demands a more dramatic location entirely.
Balance? Pah! The Joker upsets balance! The crown is nowhere near the centre — I can promise you THAT.

truth: The story always ends on the right side of the page. And this story? It ends exactly where stories end.
I am a creature of grand finales. The crown, naturally, rests at the finale — the last mask, the final answer.
In theatre, the exit is always stage right. And what a thing to find, right there at the exit.
The crown is where the last act happens. The rightmost position — the conclusion — is where conclusions live.
Follow the sequence to its end. The crown waits at the terminus, the final stop, the rightmost destination.
lie: The ending is always subverted in a Joker's tale. What feels like the final answer is never it. Start over.
You are looking too far. Too far, too final, too certain. Pull back toward the beginning.
The rightmost mask screams for attention. I would never put the crown somewhere so desperate for notice.
An obvious finale is no finale at all. The crown hides before the ending, not at it.
Do not follow the sequence to its apparent end. That path is the one I most want you to take.

moodShift: The applause fades. The lights dim. When they rise again, the Joker you knew will be gone. Something colder takes the stage.
Act One is over. The theatrical Joker bows his final bow. What replaces him does not bow. It calculates.
You enjoyed the speeches, didn't you? The grand gestures? The flair? Set that aside. Act Two has no use for warmth.
The curtain between acts is the thinnest thing in this theatre. On one side: drama. On the other: something far more dangerous.
I felt your comfort in Act One. Good. Comfort is a vulnerability. Act Two was designed to find every one of yours.
The stage transforms. The warm lights become cold. The Joker who loved language now loves only patterns — and your failure to see them.
Between acts, the mask changes. You thought you understood me. Act Two will correct that assumption, methodically and without mercy.
taunt: Rest while you can, dear player. Act Two does not believe in rest.
Did you enjoy Act One? Good. Hold onto that joy. It will not survive what comes next.
The theatrical Joker was generous. The one waiting in Act Two is not.
You think you know how this theatre works. You know nothing. Act Two will teach you.
Every smile I gave you in Act One was an investment. Act Two is where I collect.
The stage is changing. Can you feel it? That chill? That is Act Two, breathing.
I gave you warmth and language and spectacle. Now I give you patterns and silence and the cold certainty of your own limitations.

moodShift: HAHAHA! Did the cold bother you?! The patterns?! The PRECISION?! Good news — ALL OF THAT IS GONE NOW! WELCOME TO CHAOS!
Act Two is OVER! The calculating Joker steps aside! What replaces him doesn't calculate — it EXPLODES! HAHAHA!
You thought Act Two was hard?! Oh, that's PRECIOUS! Act Two had RULES! Act Three has NOTHING! HAHAHA! NOTHING AT ALL!
The transition between Two and Three is the moment the Joker stops making sense entirely. That moment? It's RIGHT NOW. HAHAHA!
Cold precision is BORING! The Joker has been BORING for a WHOLE ACT and he is DONE with it! CHAOS ARRIVES! HAHAHA!
The patterns crack! The cold shatters! What pours through the cracks is everything the Joker has been HOLDING BACK! HAHAHA!
Act Two is dead! Long live Act Three! The Joker who measured and calculated has left the building — something UNHINGED has replaced him!
taunt: You thought you had me figured out! HAHAHA! You figured out the WRONG JOKER! Meet the real one!
Act Two gave you the illusion of control. Act Three takes it away and LAUGHS about it! HAHAHA!
Did your logic survive Act Two intact? GOOD! It'll make a WONDERFUL trophy for when Act Three DESTROYS it! HAHAHA!
The calm before the storm was Act Two. Act Three IS the storm. The lightning. The HAHAHA. The beautiful EVERYTHING!
You adapted to patterns! Impressive! Can you adapt to NO patterns?! Can you adapt to CHAOS?! Find out in Act Three!
Whatever strategy brought you here — throw it away! Act Three is a NEW game and the Joker wrote the rules in invisible ink! HAHAHA!
Brace yourself! Or don't! Bracing doesn't help in a hurricane! HAHAHA! Welcome to the hurricane!

welcome: Welcome to Joker's Grand Theatre! The seats are imaginary, the danger is real, and the Joker is DELIGHTED you are here.
Ah, a visitor! The theatre opens its doors to you — and by doors, I mean jaws. Do come in, dear player.
The Grand Theatre welcomes all challengers! The foolish, the brave, the tragically overconfident — all are equally doomed here!
You stand at the entrance to the greatest show in the world. The Joker awaits inside. The question is: do you dare?
Every performance begins with an audience that believes they are safe. You are that audience. Welcome!
The marquee reads 'Joker's Grand Theatre' — and what a theatre it is! Drama! Pattern! Chaos! And YOU, the star of your own defeat!
Step right up! Step right IN! The Joker has prepared three acts of escalating madness just for someone exactly like you!
beforePlay: Three acts await you inside, dear player. Each one worse than the last. Or better! Depends entirely on your perspective!
When you are ready, the curtain rises. The Joker has been ready since before you arrived. He is always ready.
Take a breath. Gather your wits. You will need both inside — and the Joker will do his best to separate you from them.
The game begins when you say so. And when you say so, the Joker begins. And when the Joker begins, dear player — buckle up.
Inside: Act One, Act Two, Act Three. Outside: the last moment of peace you will know for a good while. Savour it.
Ready yourself for the Word Duel, the Pattern Mirror, and the Final Gamble. The Joker has prepared something special for each.
The theatre does not close. The Joker does not sleep. Begin when you must — but know that he has been waiting, and waiting makes him creative.
